reasoning: The baseline for this question corresponds to the value of the USD/JPY spot rate as of April 13. Since April 13 is a Sunday, the latest available value is from Friday, April 11, which was 143.57. Although the exchange rate experienced a strong downward trend from 158.3 in January 2025 to 143.6 in April largely due to expectations of US tariffs and shifting US-Japan yield differentials, late Friday (April 11) news indicating exclusions on some electronic components from US tariffs suggests the USD is likely to experience an upward jump ('gap up') when trading resumes on Monday, April 14. Furthermore, the FRED time series tool estimates a 70% probability of the spot rate exceeding the recent 143.6 threshold by April 20 based on linear trend expectations and seasonal comparisons. Acknowledging this quantitative anchor and the potential near-term bounce, I center my short-term probability around 0.65. Longer term, the broader bearish technical bias (with analysts noting targets around 140.00) and the random walk nature of FX markets compel me to regress the probabilities smoothly toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends to October.
